channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Boy, 14, dies from apparent self-inflicted gunshot wound in California
item.description: IRVINE (KTLA) — A 14-year-old boy in Irvine has died from an apparent self-inflicted gunshot wound that has resulted in an adult facing criminal charges. Officers and emergency medical personnel responded to a home in the area of Heathergreen and Weepingwood around 2:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration investigates Maine for claims of withholding gender transitions from parents
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The administration of President Donald Trump is investigating Maine with a claim that dozens of school districts in the state violate federal law by withholding information about students' gender transitioning from parents. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Carolina wildfire keeps growing as firefighters protect homes
item.description: A large wildfire in the South Carolina mountains has doubled in size on each of the last three days. But fire crews have been able to keep the blaze away from structures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under UN-sponsored external governance
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed Friday to temporarily put Ukraine under external governance as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement, in remarks that reflected the Kremlin leader's determination to achieve his war goals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US and Lithuanian forces struggle to drain swamp to recover 4 soldiers in submerged armored vehicle
item.description: U.S. and Lithuanian troops and authorities are struggling to drain a swampy area in Lithuania in ongoing efforts to recover four American Army soldiers who went missing three days ago and are warning that it will be a long operation.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk to travel to Wisconsin ahead of judicial race, hand out $1M checks
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk announced late Thursday he would be traveling to Wisconsin ahead of a high-stakes state Supreme Court race to talk with voters and hand out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A powerful new app for card collectors
item.description: To a card collector, there’s nothing quite like ripping open a fresh pack. “You find someone you like and you collect them… and I think it’s special,” started Jay Moslehi, also known as MOJO SPORTS on YouTube.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Skeletal remains in Fayetteville identified as man missing since 2023, police say
item.description: Police said the remains were identified as Jaylin Isaiah Dilday, who was reported missing in May 2023.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Daughter of slain Hamas hostages files suit against Columbia groups
item.description: The lawsuit claims Khalil and other student groups at Columbia helped spread Hamas messaging on Columbia University's campus.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military, the second nationwide injunction against the policy in as many weeks. The order Thursday from U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: When Nicole Taylor and her family moved to their new home in the South Carolina mountains six months ago, the gorgeous view of Table Rock Mountain was the clincher.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What we know about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: A string of crashes this year along with a near miss have brought attention to air travel, which remains overwhelmingly safe.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers in major restructuring
item.description: In a major overhaul, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers and shut down entire agencies, including ones that oversee billions of dollars in funds for addiction services and community health centers across the country.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Violin thief-turned-bank robber sentenced to 4 years in prison
item.description: A California man convicted of orchestrating a scheme to steal valuable violins, and then robbing a bank when he realized federal investigators were closing in on him, has been sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter detained by feds
item.description: A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who have expressed their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration revokes state and local health funding
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services is canceling tens of billions of dollars in federal grants that state and local health departments were using to track infectious diseases, health disparities, vaccinations, mental health services, and other health issues.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Million-dollar lottery ticket was nearly thrown out, winner says
item.description: “I was actually kind of going to throw them away, because I said ‘I’ve never won the lottery,’” said Joaquin Vaughn Redo.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge says extreme heat in Texas prisons is unconstitutional but doesn't order they install AC
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional,” but declined to order the state to immediately start installing air conditioning, which could cost billions.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump admin potential travel ban concerning airlines: Editor
item.description: "Even the announcement that they're being proposed on the list is sending a chill through the travel industry.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Washington researchers warn of serious decline in honey bee colonies in 2025
item.description: Losses of commercial honey bee colonies are projected to be the highest on record in the last decade, according to Washington State University researchers.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, KRON4

item.title: A heart-shaped note was found in socks bound for Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Someone tucked a heart-shaped note of encouragement into socks packed for Luigi Mangione to wear to court last month in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case, according to court documents made public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order from U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts to slash the federal bureaucracy. A three-judge panel on the U.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Kermit the Frog to give commencement address at University of Maryland
item.description: Prominent political figures and lawmakers on the commencement speaker circuit are about to have some friendly, froggy competition.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wildfires in North and South Carolina fueled by drought, wind and fallen trees from Hurricane Helene
item.description: Dry conditions, wind and trees downed by Hurricane Helene fueled wildfires in North Carolina and South Carolina, where evacuation orders were in effect Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director says Israeli soldiers beat him after attack by settlers
item.description: HEBRON, West Bank (AP) — Only a few weeks ago, Hamdan Ballal stood on a stage in Los Angeles accepting an Oscar for the film “No Other Land,” a documentary depicting his West Bank village’s struggle against Israel’s occupation.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FBI launches Tesla vandalism task force
item.description: The task force seeks to counter the violent activity targeting Tesla vehicles and charging stations in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: US infrastructure improved with Biden-era spending but there's a long way to go
item.description: A once-every-four-years report card on the upkeep of America's infrastructure gave it a “C” grade on Tuesday, up slightly from previous reports, largely due to investments made during former President Joe Biden's administration.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump portrait to be removed from Colorado Capitol after president's criticism
item.description: President Trump has criticized a portrait of himself hanging in the Colorado Capitol, suggesting it was intentionally distorted, while Republican lawmakers have asked for it to be removed.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, ABC 4

item.title: Jeffries calls for investigation of top Trump officials after bombshell military breach
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) is calling for Congress to investigate top Trump administration officials in the wake of a bombshell revelation that The Atlantic’s top editor was included in a group chat discussion detailing the Pentagon’s plans to strike Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Judge blocks three agencies from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday indefinitely blocked the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies, the latest roadblock to its efforts to slim down the federal bureaucracy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, ABC 4

item.title: Former Utah Rep. Mia Love, the first Black Republican woman elected to the US House, has died
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Former U.S. Rep. Mia Love of Utah, a daughter of Haitian immigrants who became the first Black Republican woman elected to Congress, died Sunday. She was 49. Love's family posted news of her death on Love's X account.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9
